items.title: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.
items.subject: Basic Supplies

sections.title: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: White and Colored Chalk
supplies.rationale:
Crayola chalk is best. Other brands tend to be less intense and full of binder that scratches the board. 

- Art and Picture Study: Used throughout art lessons and picture study for memory drawings.
- Playground Games: Used in playground games to mark the ground for hopscotch, four squares, and other games.
- Lettering: Used for chalk lettering projects.
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Picture Study
Lettering: Levels 1-2
Playground Games
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Frameless Chalkboard (18 x 24)
supplies.rationale:
- Art Instruction: Used to practice drawing forms.
- Picture Study: Used to draw memory drawings.
- Lettering: Used throughout the year.
supplies.maySub: with any 18" x 24" (or larger) chalkboard. Another option is to paint a wall or board with chalkboard paint. Chalkboard paint is listed elsewhere as an optional supply for this purpose.
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Picture Study
Lettering: Levels 1-2
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Chalkboard Paint
supplies.rationale:
- Art Instruction: Used to practice drawing forms.
- Picture Study: Used to draw memory drawings.
- Lettering: Used throughout the year.
supplies.note: Small bottles of chalkboard paint are available at hobby stores. Paint a 4x4 foot (or larger) section of wall or board with chalkboard paint. Don't forget to prep first!
supplies.maySub: with an 18” x 24” (or larger) frameless chalkboard to hang on the wall or lean on an easel. The paint is listed as an optional supply in place of the chalkboard listed in the non-optional section of art supplies.
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Picture Study
Lettering: Levels 1-2
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Acrylic Paint Multipack
supplies.rationale:
- Art History: Used for projects in Term 1.
- Geography: Used for some models throughout the lessons.
supplies.maySub: any multipack of acrylic paints.
supplies.usedInText:
Art History
Geography: Grades 3-8
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Paint Brushes for Acrylic Paint
supplies.rationale:
- Art History: Used for projects in Term 1.
- Geography: Used for some of the models throughout the lessons.
supplies.note: Acrylic paints are best when using brushes with short, stiff bristles. These should not be substituted with the natural fibers of watercolor brushes.
supplies.maySub: any paintbrush labeled as suitable for acrylic.
supplies.usedInText:
Art History
Handicrafts
Geography: Grades 3-8
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Pencil Sharpener
supplies.maySub: any pencil sharpener.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Permanent Marker
supplies.rationale:
- Art: Used in Term 3 a few times for Color Theory projects.
- Science: Primarily for marking glassware, test tubes, petri dishes, etc.
supplies.maySub: any permanent marker.
supplies.qty: 1 per student (The link is for a 4 pack.)
supplies.usedInText:
Art: Levels 2-4
Art History
Science
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Waterproof Black Ink Pen
supplies.rationale:
- Art: This pen is required for a few Level 7 art projects in Term 2.
- Other Subjects: This pen can also be used to outline and embellish Book of Centuries, architecture, and map drawings.
supplies.maySub: any waterproof black ink pen.
supplies.usedInText:
Art: Level 7
Book of Centuries
Architecture
Geography: Grade 7
High School Art Fast Track
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Child-Safe Scissors
supplies.maySub: any child-safe scissors.
supplies.usedInText: Grades 1-3
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Basic Scissors
supplies.note: There is a left-handed option. We highly recommend purchasing a good left-handed pair of scissors if you have any left-handed students. Many brands switch the handle but not the blade, leading to a lot of frustration.
supplies.maySub: any scissors.
supplies.usedInText: Grades 4+
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: White Cardstock Paper
supplies.rationale:
- Art: You will need about 5 sheets per student. Needed to print color wheel templates and, optionally, grid paper. 
- Sloyd: Cardstock is recommended for all sloyd projects in levels 1-3.
- Science: Used occasionally
supplies.maySub: any white cardstock 90 lbs or greater for science and art. May sub cardstock paper in multiple colors for sloyd.
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Science
Sloyd: Levels 1-3
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Chalkboard Eraser
supplies.maySub: any felt chalkboard eraser or use a cloth or paper towel. Baby wipes work really well too!
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Picture Study
Lettering: Levels 1-2
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Masking Tape
supplies.rationale:
- Sloyd: Used to tape down paper when measuring and laying out models on paper. 
- Science: Used occasionally for a variety of activities.
supplies.maySub: any masking or painter's tape.
supplies.usedInText:
Sloyd
Science: Grades 4-8
Art
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: 5 mm Grid Ruled Paper
supplies.rationale: Art Instruction
supplies.note: Print on cardstock as necessary.
supplies.maySub: any grid or graph paper
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Household Items - General
supplies.note: Items such as Pencils, Scissors, Scotch Tape, and Gluesticks
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Wooden Ruler with standard and metric
supplies.rationale: Used in Science, Math, and Sloyd courses for measuring and building. Can also be used to aid in map drawing for Geography.
supplies.maySub: any wooden ruler with dual measurements.
supplies.qty: Only 1 is needed most of the time, but 2 are needed for one experiment in Science: Grade 7 (Link is to 3-pack.)
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Primary Composition Notebook
supplies.rationale: Used to keep Reading Lesson lists and Copywork throughout the year and/or Written Narrations for grade 3.
supplies.maySub: any primary composition notebook.
supplies.qty: 1-2 (You can use one per subject or combine multiple subjects in one notebook.)
supplies.usedInText:
Reading Lessons
Written Narration: Grade 3
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Wide-Ruled Notebook
supplies.rationale: Used to keep student Copywork, Dictation, and Written Narration work throughout the year.
supplies.maySub: any wide ruled composition notebook.
supplies.qty: 1-3 (You can use one per subject or combine multiple subjects in one notebook.)
supplies.usedInText:
Grades 4-6
English
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Wide-Ruled & Blank Notebook
supplies.rationale: Used for drawing pictures and writing narrations about Bible stories.
supplies.maySub: any notebook that is lined and blank.
supplies.usedInText: Bible: Grades 3-6
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: College Ruled Journal
supplies.rationale: Used to keep student Bible, English, Poetry, Commonplace, and Citizenship work throughout the year.
supplies.note: Commonplace Books are often kept for years, so you might consider getting a nicer journal for that purpose.
supplies.maySub: any college-ruled notebook. A composition book like the one listed in the supplies for younger grades would work fine. Just make sure to get college-ruled instead of wide-ruled at this level.
supplies.qty: 1-5 (You can use one per subject or combine multiple subjects in one notebook.)
supplies.usedInText:
Grades 7+
Bible
Commonplace
Citizenship
English
Poetry
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Dot Grid & Blank Notebooks
supplies.rationale:
- Architecture: Used to create drawings of buildings, write narrations, and keep other notes.
- Geography: Used to draw/paste maps, write narrations, and keep other notes. 
- U.S. History: Used for written narrations and other notebook prompts.
supplies.maySub: any notebook that is blank on one side and has a dot grid on the other.
supplies.usedInText:
Architecture
Geography: Grades 2-12
US History: Grades 7-9
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Graph Paper Notebook
supplies.rationale:
- Math: Graph paper notebooks can be a helpful way for students to keep their work lined up and organized in the upper grades when worksheets are no longer used from Rightstart.
- Science: Useful for courses that employ a combination of writing, diagramming, and graphing. 
- Labs: Grades 4-8, HS Chem, Bio, Physics, and Astronomy.
- Science Lessons: Grades 8+, except HS Adv Nature Study.  We do NOT recommend using the same notebook for lessons and labs as this disorders their lesson notes and interrupts the structure and visibility of the scientific method in their lab notebook.
supplies.note: A separate notebook is needed for Math.
supplies.maySub: any notebook preferred by the student, such as quad ruled notebooks with colored paper or dot grid notebooks.
supplies.qty:
- 1 for Math
- 1 for Science Lab
- 1 for Science Lessons Grades 8+ except HS Adv Nature Study
supplies.usedInText:
Math: Grades 7+
Science: Grades 4+
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Mixed Media Pad
supplies.rationale:
- Art Instruction: Used as Art Book across all levels.
- Picture Study: Grades 7-12 use the Art Book for a few drawings.
- Lettering: The mixed media pad is used to keep lettering practice work.
- Science: Useful for courses in which there tends to be less writing and more watercolor: General Science Grades 1-3, Natural History (all grades), any Nature Notebook/Outdoor Work, High School Labs for Botany, Advanced Nature Study, Geology, and Microbiology.
supplies.maySub: any mixed media paper 90 lb or greater (Spiral-bound, 7x10 inches recommended.)
supplies.qty:
- 1-2 for Art. (May combine Picture Study with the same book as Art Instruction.)
- 1 for Lettering: Level 3
- 1 per Science student for all outdoor work. (May be shared across all science courses.)
supplies.usedInText:
Art
Picture Study
Lettering: Level 3
Science
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Book of Centuries
supplies.rationale: The Book of Centuries is used to keep important dates and view history in a more visual format to aid in making history connections.
supplies.maySub: any printable online version.
supplies.usedInText: Grades 5+
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Pencils
supplies.maySub: any pencils.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Compass
supplies.rationale:
- Art: Used for various drawing projects.
- Sloyd: Used for upper level projects requiring curved lines.
- Geography: Used for drawing and measuring larger projects and drawing maps.
- Geometry: Used for creating diagrams in various math problems.
supplies.maySub: any compass for drawing.
supplies.usedInText:
Art: Level 3
Sloyd: Levels 3-7
Geography: Grade 7
Geometry
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: 3-Ring Binder - 1"
supplies.rationale:
- Grades 1-8: May be needed if printing documents at home for the following: Math worksheets, Modern Languages, Penmanship, Science Labs (Grades 4+), Citizenship (Grades 7+)
- Grades 9-12: May be needed to house supporting documents for multiple subjects.
supplies.maySub: any binder or folders.
supplies.qty:
You may choose to get multiple small binders or combine into a larger binder with dividers.
- 2 (Grades 1-3)
- 3-4 (Grades 4-6)
- 5 (Grades 7-8)
- 1+ (Grades 9-12)
supplies.usedInText:
Citizenship
English
Science
Math
Mod. Language
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Colored Pencils
supplies.maySub: another set of Prismacolor or any other colored pencils.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Colored Reading Strips
supplies.rationale: Helpful for people with a variety of visual processing differences. This multipack provides different colors to experiment with. Once a helpful color has been identified, it can be useful to get several in that color to use as bookmarks.
supplies.note: The color is not merely for interest and 2-4 very different colors should be tried before deciding whether they are helpful or not.  Children can often identify for themselves the most helpful color, but teachers can have them read aloud for a simple assessment of whether accuracy and ease are improved.
supplies.maySub: any brand.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Composition Notebooks with Colored Paper
supplies.rationale: Helpful for children with a variety of visual processing differences.  When colored reading strips have indicated that tinted paper alleviates visual stress, the same color paper can be used to help with written work, including Copywork, Math, etc.
supplies.note: Composition notebooks with colored paper are not as readily available, so sometimes the closest color (on the color wheel) may need to be substituted.  The links provided are for 2 different colors.
supplies.maySub: any brand with an appropriate color paper.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Dividers
supplies.note: Only used if separating subjects when combining multiple subjects in one folder/binder.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Pencil Grips
supplies.note: Helps children with a variety of writing difficulties.
supplies.maySub: any comfortable grip that reinforces good finger position.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Watercolor Set
supplies.rationale: Used for Brush Drawing narrations.
supplies.note: These are not for Nature Study or Art; higher quality materials are recommended for those courses. This is a cheaper set for painting narrations in order to conserve the more expensive art-quality pigments.
supplies.maySub: any inexpensive watercolors.
supplies.usedInText: Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

supplies.title: Whiteboard
supplies.rationale: Helpful for writing exercises in Sol-fa and other subjects such as Reading lessons, Dictation, Math, drawn narrations, etc.
supplies.maySub: any chalkboard (such as the one used for art).
supplies.usedInText:
Sol-fa: Level 2
Many Subjects
programList: Used across the curriculum in 3 or more subjects.

books.title: Household Items - Art: Level 2
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Tea cup and saucer (T3)
- Basket with handle (T3)
- Conical objects like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. (T3)
- Scrap cardboard (T1)
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Hand soap or dish soap (T1/2/3)
- Towel (T1/2/3)
- Scrap paper (T1/2/3)
- Dull needle, awl, toothpick, or kabob stick (T1)
- Manipulatives/found objects (pebbles, leaves, Tangram pieces, beans, buttons, math counters, Legos, anything that you have enough of to repeat a pattern) (T3)
- White drawing/printer paper (T3)
- Marker or crayon (T3)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T3)
- Dahlia, live or cut (T2)
- Live violets (T2)

- Cloth (paper towels, old wash cloth, sponge, etc.) used to dab off excess water and to clean palettes. (T1/2/3)
- Live or cut laburnum. Can also use wisteria. (T1)
- Ivy optional (T1)
- Egg crate (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Large, heart-shaped leaves. Red buds and elephant ears are great leaves to consider. (T1)
- Towel to cover lap (T1/2/3)
- 12"x18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor or pastel paper) (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) Fast Track Drawing (T3)
- Conicals object like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3 (T3)

books.title: Household Items - Art: Level 3
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (paper towels, old wash cloth, sponge, etc.) used to dab off excess water and to clean palettes. (T1/2/3)
- Live or cut laburnum. Can also use wisteria. (T1)
- Ivy optional (T1)
- Egg crate (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Large, heart-shaped leaves. Red buds and elephant ears are great leaves to consider. (T1)
- Towel to cover lap (T1/2/3)
- 12"x18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor or pastel paper) (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) Fast Track Drawing (T3)
- Conicals object like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3 (T3)

books.title: Grid Paper Notebook
books.rationale:
For fret design. May use for brush-forms as well.
➜ Levels 1-3, Fast Track
books.maySub: grid pattern printed on white cardstock.

supplies.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
supplies.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
supplies.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.

books.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
books.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
books.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
books.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

books.title: Household Items - Art: Level 5
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Piece of fruit (apple, banana, orange, grapes, peach, kiwi, mango, etc.) (T1)
- Shoe for modeling (T1)
- Flower with leaves and stem (T1)
- Cube-shaped objects and rectangular prisms for still life. These are objects such as tissue boxes, books, picture frames, other boxes. (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T3)
- Conicals object like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3. (T3)
- Lamp or light (T2)
- Hair dryer (T3)
- Feathers, shells, or pinecones (T3)
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Towel (T1/2/3)
- Scrap cardboard
- Scrap paper

supplies.title: Household Items - Art: Level 6
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Spherical fruit (orange, lemon, apple, peach) (T1)
- Spherical objects (various balls, globe, fruits) (T1/3)
- Spray of leaves on a stem (T2)
- Objects with handles (basket, pail, pitcher) (T3)
- A couple of chairs to observe, preferably one being of a simple design (T3)
- Cupcake with frosting and sprinkles optional (T3)
- Houseplant. Grocery stores often have potted herbs in the produce section. Or pull up a weed and plant it in a cup with dirt from your yard. (T1)
- Light or lamp (T2)
- Hole punch optional (T1/2/3)
- Red cellophane sheet optional (T2)
- A flower with leaves and a stem (T2/3)

supplies.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
supplies.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
supplies.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
supplies.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

supplies.title: Household Items - Art: Level 7
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Physical copy of a black and white photo of a person from the bust up (shoulders, neck, and head) (T1)
- Trumpet and cone-shaped flowers (pansies, morning glories, lilies, beardtongue, bignonias, etc.) (T2)
- Flowers with distinct petals (vinca, clematis or daisy, etc.) It should include flower, stem with leaves, and preferably a bud. (T2)
- Cotton balls (T2)
- Bubble wrap (T2)
- White crayon (T2)
- Salt of any kind (T2)
- Flower with leaves and stem (T1)
- Scrap paper (T1/2/3)
- Lamp or light (T2/3)
- Spheres, cylinders, and cubes (T3)
- Large piece of textured (not smooth) fabric used for draping a still life. Can be a blanket, towel, large sweater or jacket, etc. (T3)
- Paper towels (T1/2/3)
- Towel (T1/2/3)

books.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
books.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
books.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
books.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

supplies.title: Household Items - Art: Fast Track
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Scrap Cardboard for base of clay (T1)
- Paper towels (T1)
- 12" x 18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor, or pastel paper) (T1)
- Scrap paper (T1/2)
- Houseplant. Grocery stores often have potted herbs in the produce section. Or pull up a weed and plant it in a cup with dirt from your yard. (T2)
- Manipulatives/found objects (can include pebbles, leaves, Tangram pieces, beans, math counters, Legos, anything that you have enough of to repeat a pattern) (T2)
- Markers or crayons (T2)
- Tea cup and saucer (can use mug and small plate) used for model (T2)
- Basket with handle used for model (T2)
- Lantern used for model. It's on pg.14 of Drawing, Design, and Craftwork. You can use a can with a funnel on top to recreate it. Or make it out of paper. (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T2)
- Conical objects like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3. (T2)
- 6x6 square piece of paper in any color (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Circle template. Trace around any object at least 4 inches in diameter: bowl, plate, lid, etc., on a sturdy piece of cardboard or cardstock. Cut it out. (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Towel (T1/2/3)

books.title: Household Items - Art: Fast Track
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Scrap Cardboard for base of clay (T1)
- Paper towels (T1)
- 12" x 18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor, or pastel paper) (T1)
- Scrap paper (T1/2)
- Houseplant. Grocery stores often have potted herbs in the produce section. Or pull up a weed and plant it in a cup with dirt from your yard. (T2)
- Manipulatives/found objects (can include pebbles, leaves, Tangram pieces, beans, math counters, Legos, anything that you have enough of to repeat a pattern) (T2)
- Markers or crayons (T2)
- Tea cup and saucer (can use mug and small plate) used for model (T2)
- Basket with handle used for model (T2)
- Lantern used for model. It's on pg.14 of Drawing, Design, and Craftwork. You can use a can with a funnel on top to recreate it. Or make it out of paper. (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T2)
- Conical objects like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3. (T2)
- 6x6 square piece of paper in any color (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Circle template. Trace around any object at least 4 inches in diameter: bowl, plate, lid, etc., on a sturdy piece of cardboard or cardstock. Cut it out. (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Towel (T1/2/3)

books.title: Grid Paper Notebook

➜ Levels 1-3, Fast Track
books.maySub: grid pattern printed on white cardstock.

supplies.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
supplies.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
supplies.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
supplies.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

Art: Level 7
Art History

supplies.title: Household Items - Art History
supplies.rationale: craft glue, scissors, , art book
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:	
- rock (to crush charcoal) (T1)
- disposable plates, bowls or cups (to mix pigments on/in and to hold flour/water mixture) (T1)
- sticks (T1)
- concrete outside (in some form to work on) (T1)
- different types and colors of paper (can be scraps) (T1)
- construction paper including a 12x18 piece of black construction paper (T1/3)
- color pencils, crayons, markers or tempera sticks (any of these will do for adding color) (T1/2/3)
- gallon sized ziplock bags (at least 2 per student) (T1)
- brown paper bag or brown craft paper (T1)
- flour (T1)
- balloon (8-12") (T1)
- newspaper, a lot for paper mache (T1)
- cardstock or cardboard (you can also recycle round oatmeal containers, toilet paper tubes, cereal boxes, etc.) (T1)
- terracotta colored paint (can be created by mixing brown or blue with orange) (T1)
- black and white paint (T1)
- sponge (T1)
- paper towels (T1/2)
- cardboard, sturdy piece for base of clay project (T1/2)
- loose white paper (i.e. drawing paper, rice paper, copy paper, newsprint) (T1)
- styrofoam or porcelain plate for India ink pallette (T1)
- styrofoam or glass sheet (can use styrofoam plate or sheet of glass or plexiglass from old picture frame) (T1)
- watercolors and palette (T1/3)
-  8x8" square of multimedia paper (cut this out of your art book) (T2)
- circle templates (optional) (T2)
- ball point pen (T2)
- stylus or awl/needle tool (T2)
- plastic spoon (optional) (T2/3)
- egg (T2)
- old muffin tin, plastic egg carton or paint palette for mixing colors in (T2)
- Art book or loose mixed media paper (T1/2/3)
- old bowl or plate for grinding pastels (T2)
- two sheets of cardboard measuring 11x14, can be recycled from boxes or packaging (T2)
- glass jar or glass sheet like from 8x10 picture frame (T2/3)
- cloth (T1/2/3)

books.note: Only used in 2 lessons of Drawing Level 4. Used more heavily in Levels 5-8 and High School Fast Track.
books.maySub: any pack of compressed charcoal.

Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- An orange (you'll need one a couple of separate times) (T1)
- Small object like car, lego figure, action figure, doll (T1)
- Ball for student to try to spin on finger (optional) (T1)
- Lamp, flashlight, light (T1)
- Blocks or Legos to build a model of a room (T1/2)
- Penny or other small object (T1)
- Small sticker or sticky note (T1)
- Object like stuffed animal, toy, kitchen utensil, brush, something that can be hidden and found. Will be used outside in one lesson. (T1) 
- Balloon, basic round latex (T1)
- Paved area (T2)
- Treat like might be found in a treasure hunt (snack, small toy, or gift of some kind) (T2)
- Pan or tray that will hold water (T3)
- Pot or tea kettle for boiling water (T3)
- Jar for measuring rainfall. Wide mouth is best. (T3) 
- Long art paper or a few pieces of paper taped together end to end (T3)
- Glue or tape (T3)

- Science: For making a model of a pond habitat in Term 1.

supplies.title: Household Items - Geography: Grade 3
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Clay (Model Magic or homemade recipe is at end of Geography lessons in appendix) (T1/2)
- Dental floss (T1) 
- A sturdy base to trace an outline map and build a model on (cardstock, cardboard, etc)(T1/2)
- Tool to carve clay with (toothpick, kitchen knife, clay modeling tools, etc.) (T1/2)
- Two pieces of cork or some other floating object (T1)
- A timer with seconds (phone is fine) (T1)
- Street map of your city. Can often find online and print off. (T2)
- Watering can or something to pour water slowly and gently (T2)
- Shallow pan like cake pan or cookie sheet with sides. (T3)
- Rock or other large heavy item that is deeper than your shallow pan. Could use an inverted bowl. (T3)

supplies.maySub: any sponge.

supplies.title: Household Items - Handicrafts
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- water container(s) (cottage cheese container, cup, bowl, jar) (optional in T2) (2 needed in T3)
- rolling pin (can use any cylinder that will roll clay such as pvc pipe, wooden dowel, etc.) (T2)
- onion (T2)
- a pepper of any kind (T2)
- seashell (T2)
- table covering (used to protect the surface of your work area and make clean up simpler; can use newspaper, old sheet or tablecloth, old vegetable or bread cutting boards, etc.) (T2)
- paper towels or cloth (for cleanup and keeping in-progress clay projects wet) (T2)
- plastic bag or container with air-tight lid (T2)

supplies.title: Clear Glaze Aerosol Spray
supplies.rationale: Used to add glaze to clay pieces.

supplies.title: Silicone Pottery Clay Sculpt Tools
supplies.rationale: Used for smoothing and shaping the clay.

books.title: Household Items - Handicrafts
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- water container(s) (cottage cheese container, cup, bowl, jar) (optional in T2) (2 needed in T3)
- rolling pin (can use any cylinder that will roll clay such as pvc pipe, wooden dowel, etc.) (T2)
- onion (T2)
- a pepper of any kind (T2)
- seashell (T2)
- table covering (used to protect the surface of your work area and make clean up simpler; can use newspaper, old sheet or tablecloth, old vegetable or bread cutting boards, etc.) (T2)
- paper towels or cloth (for cleanup and keeping in-progress clay projects wet) (T2)
- plastic bag or container with air-tight lid (T2)

supplies.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
supplies.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
supplies.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
supplies.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

supplies.rationale: Students will use brush pens to accomplish lettering skills.
supplies.maySub: any brush pens.

books.title: Beste Golden Taklon Round Brush Set
books.rationale: Recommend Beste because they hold up well and economically priced. This set of 6 offers versatility. Students can use the four biggest sizes for lessons, then appropriate sizes for practicing. Should last for years if cared for properly.
books.note: Watercolors are best when using brushes with natural fibers. These should not be substituted with the short, stiff bristles of acrylic brushes. Watercolor brushes may be used as a substitute for bamboo brushes used for Sumi-e painting in Art History.
books.maySub: any Beste brand golden taklon hair brush. Need at least a size 8 and 12 round for brush drawing.

- 1 gallon jug of milk or water, the type with a handle

supplies.title: Household Items - General Science: Grade 8
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• 5 Starburst candies or similarly-shaped manufactured item
• sturdy wooden board at least 2-3" wide and 3.5' long to construct marble ramp
• anything to create rails for the ramp, more scrap wood, old pool noodles, etc.
• whatever is needed to attach the rails: bar clamps, hammer and nails, glue, etc.
• books or wood blocks to adjust height
• 3 smaller blocks to act as stops 
• 1-6 eggs (Term 1)
• water
• large sheet pan or flat location outside
• 2 plastic cups, 1 smaller and 1 larger
• various objects to test for sliding on ramp, such as small objects made of wood, plastic, metal, etc
• various Lego-type blocks that can be used to build a small box, roughly 1-2" dimensions 
• a larger bouncy ball, such as a basketball
• a location to bounce balls
• 1/2 c small solid fill material, such as cat food or rice
• 2 locations at 2 different temperatures, such as a sunny window and a chilly basement
• plastic wrap
• wire cutters, such as those on many pliers
• hammer
• at least 2 different light sources, including bright sunlight and at least 1 type of artificial light
• microwave
• microwave-safe casserole dish, at least 9"
• marshmallows to cover the bottom of the dish, Peeps or jumbo work best
• stopwatch, such as found on most electronic devices
• calculator, such as found on most electronic devices
• printables
• computer
• optional: hot glue gun
• optional: long forceps
• optional: matches
• optional: glass jar that is taller than a tea light candle
• optional: hard boiled egg (Term 2)

supplies.title: Prepared Microscope Slides
supplies.rationale: 25 prepared slides, including plants, insects, and animal tissues.  Scheduled in Grade 8 Natural History, high school Biology, and high school Botany, but fun to explore in grades 6+.
supplies.maySub: any variety set that includes the same/similar mounts; check lesson plans for the relevant subject for details.

supplies.title: Alveary High School Chemistry Kit
supplies.rationale: Supplies harder-to-find materials for labs and lessons.  General safety information is provided by manufacturer(s) and in the lab books, but teachers should educate themselves regarding local regulations for safe disposal of any materials, especially lab or industrial chemicals.
supplies.note: Canadian members: The Learning House is able to provide the HST kits in Canada.
supplies.maySub: comparable items, if purchased or found separately; refer to kit contents at HST website for a list of supplies included in the kits and/or lessons/lab books for a comprehensive list of supplies needed for each lab.
supplies.qty: 1 kit for 1-3 students (Could stretch to 4, if needed.)

supplies.title: Household Items - Science: Chemistry
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• 3 small objects
• distilled or deionized water 
• mineral spirits/paint thinner (may substitute mineral oil or cooking oil, but won't be able to evaporate the solvent at the end of the lab)
• 4 recycled soda cans
• sticky tack, a piece of foam, or anything to hold thermometer in soda can and close the opening
• hydrogen peroxide antiseptic
• barometer or computer access to check local weather station
• ice
• sugar
• salt
• spoon
• lemon juice
• stopwatch, such as those found on most electronic devices
• microwave-safe bowl 
• microwave
• whisk that you do not care about
• another whisk or electric mixer
• well-ventilated space (porch, carport, etc)
• juice or tea
• quart-sized mason jar with a lid
• jar, cup, or bowl
• slotted spoon, strainer, or coffee filter
• 2 large egg whites (Term 3)
• large mixing bowl
• cookie sheet
• oven
• optional: hard blunt tip (knitting needle, skewer, etc)
• optional: 227g coconut oil from the grocery store, IF you want to use the soap you will make
• optional: lemon-lime soda, ice cream, yogurt, or cottage cheese (Term 3)
• optional: cream of tartar
• optional: piping bag
• optional: food coloring
• optional: parchment paper

supplies.title: Organic Chemistry Model Kit
supplies.rationale: Helps students accurately visualize the three-dimensional structure of simple molecules.
supplies.maySub: any molecule modeling kit.  Marshmallows and toothpicks can also be used, but are not self-correcting like the kits.

supplies.title: Food-Grade Calcium Chloride
supplies.rationale: The calcium chloride supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and NOT for consumption.  If students would like to eat the dessert caviar they make, they should obtain a food-grade chemical.
supplies.qty: 1 for 1-4 students (possibly more depending on interest)

supplies.title: Food-Grade Sodium Alginate
supplies.rationale: The sodium alginate supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and NOT for consumption.  If students would like to eat the dessert caviar they make, they should obtain a food-grade chemical.
supplies.qty: 1 for 1-4 students (possibly more depending on interest)

supplies.title: Food-Grade Sodium Hydroxide
supplies.rationale: The sodium hydroxide supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and is not for skin contact use.  If students would like to produce soap for use, then a food-grade chemical should be purchased.
supplies.qty: 1 for 1-4 students (possibly more depending on interest)

supplies.title: Safety Goggles
supplies.rationale: Used for eye protection in Science Labs for grades 4+.
supplies.note: Safety goggles are included in the HST Alveary kits for grades 5 and high school Chemistry.
supplies.maySub: any well-fitting safety glasses/goggles.
supplies.qty: 1 per student (If students are not doing lab at the same time, they could share.)

supplies.title: Elodea
supplies.note: Elodea is invasive in some areas and cannot be disposed of outside; it must be discarded in the trash or used in an aquarium when finished.
supplies.maySub: another water plant with many thin leaves.

supplies.title: Household Items - Science: Biology
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• any living plant specimen found nearby
• any non-living specimen 
• red onion (Term 1)
• kitchen knife
• distilled or deionized water
• paper towels
• lamp or bright light
• strawberries (Term 1)
• salt
• a variety of colors of small Lego bricks
• computer access
• stopwatch, such as those found on most electronic devices
• cereal O's 
• small bread cubes or mini marshmallows
• chopsticks

supplies.title: Frog Dissection Specimen
supplies.rationale: High School biology students complete a frog dissection as part of a series to learn about introductory anatomy and physiology.
supplies.note: Due to a shortage, a limited number of Alveary Biology kits will include this item.  IF you need to purchase this item separately, teachers will need to obtain this item for Term 3.
supplies.maySub: any preserved frog specimen

supplies.qty: 1 per student (If students are not doing lab at the same time, they could share.)

books.title: Alveary High School Biology Kit
books.rationale: Supplies harder-to-find materials for labs and lessons.  General safety information is provided by manufacturer(s) and in the lab books, but teachers should educate themselves regarding local regulations for safe disposal of any materials, especially lab or industrial chemicals.
books.note:
There is a shortage on frog dissection specimens.  HST has a limited number of Alveary kits that include this item.  Once those kits are gone, they will adjust the price.  This specimen is not needed to get started, but teachers will need to obtain this item separately if not included.

Canadian members: The Learning House is able to provide the HST kits in Canada.
books.maySub: comparable items, if purchased or found separately; refer to kit contents at HST website for a list of supplies included in the kits and/or lessons/lab books for a comprehensive list of supplies needed for each lab.
books.qty: 1 kit for 1-3 students (Could stretch to 4, if needed.)

books.title: Elodea
books.note: Elodea is invasive in some areas and cannot be disposed of outside; it must be discarded in the trash or used in an aquarium when finished.
books.maySub: another water plant with many thin leaves.

books.title: Household Items - Science: Biology
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• any living plant specimen found nearby
• any non-living specimen 
• red onion (Term 1)
• kitchen knife
• distilled or deionized water
• paper towels
• lamp or bright light
• strawberries (Term 1)
• salt
• a variety of colors of small Lego bricks
• computer access
• stopwatch, such as those found on most electronic devices
• cereal O's 
• small bread cubes or mini marshmallows
• chopsticks
• fork

supplies.title: Household Items - Science: Botany
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• various plants specimens, found or obtained as detailed in Elementary Plant Studies
• lily flower (Term 1)
• found twigs and fruits/seeds
• needle and/or blade for dissecting
• forceps/tweezers for dissecting
• large, transparent cup/glass
• masking tape
• aluminum foil
• 2 glass jars
• salt
• oil
• microscope coverslips and slides
• paper towels
• saucer
• recycled box
• pins
• cotton ball
• various corks
• recycled measuring spoon/cup marked in tsp/Tbsp or mL
• recycled apple corer or trowel
• place to dig soil
• distilled water
• zip top sandwich bags for soil samples
• small jars with secure lid for each soil sample
• dropper
• pea/bean seeds
• potting materials
• denatured alcohol
• iodine
• potato
• matches 
• candle
• red food coloring/dye
• straw
• materials by student choice
• optional: tulip bulb
